mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals a labor market contraction that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in labor contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.1 million units signals a surge in demand for construction materials and labor, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in housing boom periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 99 signals a supply chain disruption that reduces corporate profits and stock prices, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply chain disruption periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3.5% signals a strong demand for goods and services, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strong demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial jobless claims below 250,000 signals a strong labor market, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strong labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 4.5% signals a strong demand for goods and services, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strong demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.
